hypothesis: If an autonomous materials discovery agent uses a pretrained structure generator (MatterGen) coupled with a diversity-based acquisition function, then within a 20-query budget it will discover more novel ε-stable (≤0.01 eV/atom) compounds than agents using random generation and/or random acquisition across Na–O and Co–Nb–Sn systems, because guided generation and diversity maximization more efficiently explore composition and structure space under the ORB-v3 oracle.
